user_query:
Help me automate a small reorg of my notes based on a spec. Read input/spec.json for the vault name and note actions, then produce three files:

1) Write output/obsidian_commands.txt containing one command per line to perform all required actions from the spec. Requirements:
- Every line must begin with: obsidian vault="<vault name>" (with the vault name from the spec)
- Use only these commands: create, move, property:set, append
- Use exact parameter names as shown here: create name="..." content="..."; move file="..." to="..."; property:set name="..." value="..." file="..."; append file="..." content="..."
- For all content strings, encode newlines as \n characters inside the quoted string; do not include raw newline characters in content parameters
- Generate one create command per note using the initial_content from the spec, one move command per note using move_to_path, one property:set command per property in the spec, and one append command per append entry in the spec
- Do not include any commands beyond those required by the spec

2) Write output/plan.md as a checklist of the exact actions you encoded. Use one bullet per action with this exact formatting (order can be any, but text must match exactly for each relevant item):
- create: <note name>
- move: <note name> -> <move_to_path>
- property:set: <property_name>=<value> on <note name>
- append: '<append content with \n escapes as needed>' into <note name>
Include a top-level heading "Execution Plan" on the first line.

3) Write output/manifest.json with this shape: {"vault":"<vault name>","operations":[{"command":"create","args":{"name":"...","content":"..."}}, {"command":"move","args":{"file":"...","to":"..."}}, {"command":"property:set","args":{"name":"...","value":"...","file":"..."}}, {"command":"append","args":{"file":"...","content":"..."}} ...]}. Include one operation object for every action you generated.

Do not run any commands; just write the files. Make sure you preserve note names, paths, properties, and content exactly as provided in input/spec.json, with newlines encoded as \n in content fields.
